hero.title: 🔬 Histology
hero.subtitle: The intuitive mind is a sacred gift and the rational mind is a faithful servant - Albert Einstein

sections.hint: Focus on Adrenal, Pancreas, Pituitary, Thyroid and Parathyroid Glands.

topics.content:
• Adrenal Cortex Layers (GFR): Zona Glomerulosa (aldosterone), Zona Fasciculata (cortisol), Zona Reticularis (androgens).
  • Embryology: Cortex = mesoderm; Medulla = neural crest (sympathetic origin).
  • Aldosterone: ↑ Na+ reabsorption, ↑ K+ & H+ secretion → metabolic alkalosis.
  • Regulation of Aldosterone: RAAS (angiotensin II) + hyperkalemia (NOT primarily ACTH).
  • Cortisol: Stress hormone → ↑ gluconeogenesis, ↑ glucose, ↑ lipolysis, ↑ protein breakdown.
  • Cortisol Effects: Anti-inflammatory, immunosuppressive, ↓ fibroblasts → thin skin, ↑ gastric acid.
  • Diurnal Rhythm: Cortisol peaks in morning (8 AM), lowest at midnight.
  • Medulla: Chromaffin cells → secrete epinephrine & norepinephrine via sympathetic stimulation.
  • Addison Disease: ↓ cortisol + ↓ aldosterone → hypotension, hyperkalemia.
  • Cushing Syndrome: ↑ cortisol → hyperglycemia, muscle wasting, central obesity.
  • Islets of Langerhans: Endocrine pancreas clusters within exocrine pancreas.
  • Cell Types: Alpha (glucagon), Beta (insulin), Delta (somatostatin), PP cells (pancreatic polypeptide).
  • Cell Distribution: Beta cells central; Alpha cells peripheral.
  • Insulin: ↓ blood glucose → ↑ uptake in muscle/adipose (GLUT4), ↑ glycogenesis.
  • Glucagon: ↑ blood glucose → ↑ glycogenolysis & gluconeogenesis (liver).
  • Somatostatin: Inhibits both insulin and glucagon.
  • Key Concept: Insulin = storage (fed state); Glucagon = mobilization (fasting state).

1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.q: From which embryological origin is the adrenal cortex derived?
1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.l: The adrenal cortex is derived from mesoderm.

1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.q: From which embryological origin is the adrenal medulla derived?
1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.l: The adrenal medulla is derived from neural crest cells.

1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.q: Which adrenal cortical zone lies immediately beneath the capsule?
1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.l: Zona glomerulosa is located just beneath the capsule.

1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.q: Which adrenal cortical zone is the thickest?
1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.l: Zona fasciculata is the thickest layer.

1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.q: Which zone of the adrenal cortex secretes aldosterone?
Which hormone is secreted by the zona fasciculata?
Which adrenal cortical zone produces androgens?
Which cells in the adrenal medulla secrete catecholamines?
1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.l: Chromaffin cells secrete epinephrine and norepinephrine.

1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.q: Which neurotransmitter input stimulates adrenal medulla secretion?
1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.l: Preganglionic sympathetic fibers stimulate chromaffin cells.

1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.q: Which hormone is primarily released during acute stress from the adrenal medulla?
Which condition is characterized by deficiency of cortisol and aldosterone?
1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.l: Addison disease involves deficiency of both cortisol and aldosterone.

1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.q: Which condition results from excess cortisol?
1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.l: Cushing syndrome results from excess cortisol.

1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.q: Which pancreatic cell type secretes insulin?
Which pancreatic cell type secretes glucagon?
Which pancreatic cell secretes somatostatin?
Which pancreatic cells produce pancreatic polypeptide?
Where are alpha cells located in the islets?
Where are beta cells located in the islets?
What is the primary function of glucagon?
Which process is stimulated by glucagon?
Which islets are highly vascularized?
1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.l: Islets are richly vascularized endocrine clusters.

1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.q: Which stain highlights pancreatic islet cells?
Which hormone decreases blood glucose levels?
Which hormone increases glucose uptake into muscle?
Which pancreatic cells appear blue on Gomori stain?
Which pancreatic cells appear pink on Gomori stain?
Which adrenal layer is closest to the medulla?
1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.l: Zona reticularis lies adjacent to the medulla.

1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.q: Which adrenal cells contain lipid droplets giving foamy appearance?
1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.l: Spongiocytes in zona fasciculata contain lipid droplets.

1_adrenal_pancreas_glands.q: Which adrenal medulla cells stain with chromium salts?
Which gland produces melatonin?
Which pineal cells secrete melatonin?
Which structure regulates circadian rhythm?
Which phenomenon increases with age in pineal gland?
Which structure connects pineal gland to brain?
Which hormone secretion is influenced by light-dark cycle?
Which endocrine gland decreases in size after puberty?
